description_long: This is a rectangular, industrial-style air conditioner unit elevated on two sturdy metal legs. The front face features a large, circular fan grille with a spiral wire pattern, surrounded by a white metal casing that is covered in various colorful stickers and graffiti, giving it a distinctly urban, weathered appearance. The top surface is flat and also heavily adorned with stickers and tags, suggesting it is located in a public or high-traffic area. The rear side displays a wide, horizontal heat exchanger grille with visible wear and dents, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. The sides of the unit are marked with more stickers, faded labels, and some chipped paint, further emphasizing its utilitarian and well-used character. The overall construction appears robust, with the main body likely made of painted steel and internal components of metal and plastic, designed to withstand outdoor conditions.
description: A rectangular, sticker-covered air conditioner unit on metal legs, featuring a large circular fan grille and a weathered, urban appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows a prominent circular fan grille with a spiral pattern, surrounded by a white, sticker-covered surface and graffiti.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals faded stickers, a large black-and-white label, and chipped paint, with the unit's depth and metal legs visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a wide, horizontal heat exchanger grille with visible dents and wear, and more stickers on the top surface.
description_view_3: The right side view shows additional faded stickers, labels, and chipped paint, with the metal legs and the unit's rectangular profile visible.
attribute_grille_pattern: The front grille has a circular, spiral wire pattern with closely spaced concentric rings radiating from the center.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: The exhaust vent is a large, rectangular horizontal grille on the rear, with parallel slats and visible wear.
